diagnostics.interpretation: Décalage systématique entre campagnes, instruments ou référence blanche.
diagnostics.label: Erreur calibration / référence blanche

diagnostics.interpretation: Effet systématique du support, blanc/noir, transflectance ou environnement de mesure.
diagnostics.label: Fond différent

diagnostics.interpretation: Artefacts numériques ou traitement spectral incorrect.
diagnostics.label: Erreur interpolation / rééchantillonnage

diagnostics.interpretation: Combinaison possible changement de sonde + splice, amplifiée par géométrie, fond ou calibration.

diagnostics.interpretation: Modification de l'illumination, collecte, angle ou distance sonde-échantillon.

diagnostics.interpretation: Variété, espèce, lot ou condition différente mais physiquement plausible.
diagnostics.label: Spectre hors domaine valide

diagnostics.interpretation: Mélange de campagnes, opérateurs, lots, setups ou sous-populations spectrales.
diagnostics.label: Dataset multi-régimes

top_classes.name: Sample from Horenec, Bilina, Cechy, Czechoslovakia, viaSmithsonian (sample no. NMNH B10083).

Smithsonian NMNH 134700Sample from Palermo Mine, NorthGroton, New Hampshire.
Sample from Orijarvi, Finland via the Smithsonian (sampleno. NMNH 78228).
Sao Jose De Brejuada, Minas Gerais, Brazil via the Smithsonian(sample no. NMNH 94217-1)
Minas Gerais, Brazil via the Hunt and Salisbury Collection#120B (purchased from Ward's Natural Science Establishment)
Narsarsuk, Greenland via the Smithsonian (sample no. NMNH95506).

The sample was composed of two transparent, colorless pieces: one prismatic, 1.8 cm x 1.8 cm x 2 cm, and weighing about 2 g, the other a 5 mm x 5 mm x 5 mm cleavage fragment weighing 0.65 g. No impurities were detected in hand sample or microscopically. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.aragon1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of transparent euhedral crystals. The largest was about 2 cm x 2 cm. This was obviously pure optical quality calcite. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.calcit1.spectrum.txt

No solid sample is available for the specimen. Microscopic examination of the white powder fraction indicated pure calcite. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.calcit2.spectrum.txt

The sample was a light tan irregular mass and appeared to be a pure carbonate, both in hand specimen and under the microscope. Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.ceruss1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a single white crystal, 3 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm and 29 g, showing good cleavage. No impurities or alteration were visible in hand sample. Under the microscope it appears very pure and unaltered. Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.dolomi1.spectrum.txt

The sample appeared to be pure white dolomite in hand specimen and under the microscope. Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.dolomi2.spectrum.txt

The hand sample was massive, multicrystalline, and pink in color and appeared to be pure dolomite. Microscopic examination confirmed this. Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.carbonate.none.powder.dolomi3.spectrum.txt

The sample was a large piece of a transparent single crystal and appeared to be pure fluorite. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.halide.none.powder.flouri1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of a 4 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm cluster of light brown radiating acicular crystals weighing 10 g. No impurities were observed in hand sample or under the microscope. Particle size was less than 2 micrometers.(Goethite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.hydroxide.none.powder.goethi1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a 2 cm x 2 cm x 2 cm layered botryoidal fragment weighing 14.5 g. Its surface was glossy black, with interior layers various shades of yellow-brown. No impurities were noted in hand sample or microscopically. Particle size was less than 2 micrometers.(Goethite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.hydroxide.none.powder.goethi2.spectrum.txt

The sample was a 2.5 cm x 2.5 cm x 1 cm sawn plate, deep red in color and weighing 15.3 g. It appears clean with no impurities in hand sample or microscopically. Particle size was less than 2 micrometers.(Hematite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.powder.hemati1.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of two large and several smaller fragments. Each large piece was about 1 cm x 3 cm, with a total weight of 19 g. Many surfaces of this black mineral had a yellow-brown alteration coating, which was difficult to remove completely. Under the microscope the sample appeared pure. Particle size was Less than 2 micrometers.(Hematite Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.powder.ilmeni1.spectrum.txt

The sample was composed of two large (3 cm x 3 cm x 2 cm) fragments of a single crystal, weighing 26.7 g. The crystal fragments were dark gray in color, with good crystal faces, all of which had a red-brown, earthy material adhering in places. These coatings were removed by ultrasonic cleaning. Particle size was Less than 2 micrometers.(Spinel Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.powder.magnet1.spectrum.txt

The sample was composed of three small crystals with a total weight of 3.83 g. Some reddish-brown clay mineral and muscovite coating one surface was scraped away. Veining inside crystals showed limonitic staining. The crushed sample was given an HCl and HF bath to remove stain, after which microscopic examination indicated a pure sample. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(Rutile Group) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.oxide.none.powder.rutile1.spectrum.txt

The hand sample was cut and polished along an unknown axis. It was pale green and appeared pure. Microscopic examination showed a small amount (2% or less) of very fine, low birefringent material in the sample. The contaminating grains occasionally have rhombic or cubic shape. These grains were unidentifiable. Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers. (Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.powder.beryl1.spectrum.txt

Hand sample was pale green to white in color and appeared pure on the inside, with a small amount of surface discoloration. The sample was roughly cubical, 2 cm on a side. Microscopic examination indicated it to be pure with a very small amount (much less than 1%) of alteration. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.powder.beryl2.spectrum.txt

The sample consists of three pieces, each about 2 cm x 4 cm x 1 cm. There are some heterogeneities in color, but the samples are predominantly blue-gray. They appear fairly pure, but do have a few veins with discoloration along them. Under the petrographic microscope all samples appear to be cordierite, but grains have a small amount of alteration and some microcrystalline inclusions. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.powder.cordie2.spectrum.txt

The sample was a purple, translucent, sudhedral crystal, with some green coloration at one end. The crystal was 5 mm x 2 cm x 2 cm in size. Microscopic examination indicated that the mineral was pure. Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers.(Ring Silicates) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.powder.tourma1.spectrum.txt

The sample was a translucent, blue, single crystal. It appeared quite pure under the petrographic microscope.(Ring Silicates)Particle size was Less than 2 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.cyclosilicate.powder.tourma2.spectrum.txt

The sample consisted of black radiating acicular crystals with some contaminating brown coating, which dissolved in dilute HCl. Sample size was 2 cm x 2 cm x 1 cm. Contamination was largely eliminated by hand picking and the little remaining was dissolved with dilute HCl. Particle size was less than 2 Micrometers.(Pyroxene Group - single chain) Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.nicolet.mineral.silicate.inosilicate.powder.acmite1.spectrum.txt
